description:
There are a number of methods to access the root account directly.
Without a password set any user would be able to gain access and
thus control over the entire system.
rationale: Access to root should be secured at all times.
ocil_clause: root password is not set or is not locked
ocil:
Run the following command to verify that either the root user's password is set or the root user's account is locked: 
# passwd -S root | awk '$2 ~ /^(P|L)/ {print "User: \"" $1 "\" Password is status: " $2}'
Verify the output is either: User: "root" Password is status: P - OR - User: "root" Password is status: L Note: - P - Password is set - L - Password is locked

warnings.general: This rule doesn't come with a remediation, as the exact requirement allows root to either have a password or be locked.

title: Ensure root account access is controlled
